title: Welcome to Spain
description: First day in Spain, meeting your roommate Alex

dialog.speaker: narrator
dialog.text: You wake up in your new apartment in Spain. The morning sunlight streams through the blinds as you hear someone in the kitchen.

dialog.speaker: player
dialog.text: Good morning. You must be Alex?

options.text: Good morning. You must be Alex?

dialog.speaker: alex
dialog.text: That's right! Welcome to Spain! I'm Alex Thompson, your roommate for the month. I'm from Canada, but I've been living here for a couple of years.
dialog.spanish: ¡Así es! Bienvenido a España. Soy Alex Thompson, tu compañero de piso por este mes. Soy de Canadá, pero llevo un par de años viviendo aquí.

dialog.speaker: player
dialog.text: Thanks for having me. I'm excited to be here.

options.text: Thanks for having me. I'm excited to be here.
options.spanish: Gracias por recibirme. Estoy emocionado de estar aquí.

dialog.speaker: alex
dialog.text: I made some coffee. Would you like some before we head out? Your language school orientation starts at 10:00.
dialog.spanish: He hecho café. ¿Quieres antes de que salgamos? La orientación de tu escuela de idiomas empieza a las 10:00.

dialog.speaker: alex
dialog.text: It's about 8:30 now. The school is a 15-minute walk from here. I'll show you the way today.
dialog.spanish: Son las 8:30 aproximadamente. La escuela está a 15 minutos a pie desde aquí. Te mostraré el camino hoy.

vocabulary.example: ¡Buenos días! ¿Cómo estás? (Good morning! How are you?)
Mucho gusto, soy María. (Nice to meet you, I'm María.)
Gracias por el café. (Thanks for the coffee.)
¿Qué hora es ahora? (What time is it now?)

grammar.explanation: Basic present-tense forms and simple polite structures useful for beginners
Phrases to indicate origin or distance

examples.spanish: La escuela está a 15 minutos caminando desde aquí.
examples.translation: The school is a 15-minute walk from here.

culturalNotes.title: Greetings and formality
culturalNotes.content: In Spanish, greetings and formality vary by region. 'Buenos días' is used in the morning, 'Buenas tardes' in the afternoon, and you can use 'usted' vs 'tú' depending on formality. Encourage learners to notice register and context.

culturalNotes.title: Coffee culture
culturalNotes.content: Cafés are social hubs in many Spanish-speaking regions. Use café scenes for casual conversation practice and to introduce food/drink vocabulary.

exercises.instructions: Match the Spanish words with their English meanings
Fill in the blanks with appropriate Spanish responses
